purpose: Realistic test doubles for ultrainit's two-pass synthesis phase (Phase 4). The three JSON files represent the structured output from Pass 1 (documentation: CLAUDE.md files), Pass 2 (tooling: skills, hooks, subagents, MCP servers), and the final merged artifact — exactly what `lib/synthesize.sh` writes to `.ultrainit/synthesis/` during a real run. Tests load these fixtures via the mock Claude dispatcher to exercise `merge.sh`, `validate.sh`, and `synthesize.sh` without making real API calls.

architecture.overview: Three files mirror the three intermediate files ultrainit writes to .ultrainit/synthesis/ during Phase 4. output-docs.json is Pass 1 output (consumed by synthesis-docs.json schema), output-tooling.json is Pass 2 output (synthesis-tooling.json schema), and output.json is the merged result produced by `jq -s '.[0] * .[1]'` on the first two. The split reflects the architectural decision to separate documentation generation from tooling generation so each synthesis pass can focus deeply on its domain.

subdirectories.purpose: Contains the three synthesis fixture files — no subdirectories. Kept flat because each file corresponds to a distinct, well-defined pipeline stage.

architecture.data_flow: Tests copy fixtures into $WORK_DIR/synthesis/ (unit tests) or wrap them in a mock Claude JSON envelope via make_claude_envelope() (integration tests). In unit tests, merge.sh functions read output.json directly. In integration tests, mock_claude.bash dispatches per-agent response files keyed by agent name, so the synthesis-docs and synthesis-tooling passes each receive their respective fixture wrapped in {is_error, total_cost_usd, structured_output}. synthesize.sh then calls merge_synthesis_passes() which runs jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' to produce output.json — validating the merge logic end-to-end.

key_files.purpose: Final merged synthesis artifact. Contains all 7 top-level keys: claude_md, subdirectory_claude_mds, skills, hooks, subagents, mcp_servers, settings_hooks. This is what merge.sh reads to write .claude/ artifacts to disk. Used by merge.bats for write_artifacts(), merge_settings(), and write_mcp_config() tests.

Pass 1 fixture. Contains only claude_md (multi-line markdown string, ~23 lines) and subdirectory_claude_mds (array with one entry for 'src'). Used by synthesize_helpers.bats and integration/synthesize_phase.bats to simulate the first synthesis pass.

Pass 2 fixture. Contains skills (2 entries: api-development, database-migrations), hooks (1 entry: format-on-save.sh), subagents (empty array), mcp_servers (1 entry: context7), and settings_hooks (1 entry). Used by integration/synthesize_phase.bats to simulate the second synthesis pass.

patterns.name: Embedded Markdown in JSON Strings
patterns.description:
All multi-line content (CLAUDE.md, skill bodies, hook scripts, subagent markdown) is stored as a single JSON string with literal 
 characters. YAML frontmatter for skills/subagents is embedded inline: '---
name: api-development
description: ...
---
## Key Files
...'. Consumers (merge.sh) write these strings directly to disk. When writing fixture content, use actual newlines escaped as 
 — do not split into arrays.

patterns.name: Intentionally Minimal Fixture Data
patterns.description: Fixtures deliberately use far fewer entries than a real synthesis would produce (2 skills instead of 10-30, 1 subdirectory CLAUDE.md instead of 5-15, 0 subagents). This keeps test execution fast and assertions simple. When adding new fixture data for a new test, add only the fields needed to exercise the code path under test — do not try to make fixtures 'realistic' in size.

patterns.name: 1:1 Hook-to-Settings Wiring
patterns.description: Every entry in hooks[] must have a corresponding entry in settings_hooks[]. The hooks[i].filename (e.g. 'format-on-save.sh') maps to settings_hooks[i].command ('.claude/hooks/format-on-save.sh'). validate.sh enforces this: if hooks is non-empty, settings_hooks must be non-empty. The fixture demonstrates this: one hook entry paired with one settings_hooks entry. When adding a hook to the fixture, always add its settings_hooks counterpart.

patterns.name: Skill Description Safety Rules
patterns.description: Skill description strings must NOT contain angle brackets (< >) because they break YAML parsing when the skill content file is written. The postprocess_descriptions() function in synthesize.sh replaces them with quotes. The fixture demonstrates the correct form: 'Use when building or modifying API endpoints in src/api/. Covers route creation, middleware wiring, and request validation. Do NOT use for frontend components.' — natural language, no angle brackets, includes at least 3 trigger phrases and one 'Do NOT use for' boundary.

patterns.name: Mock Claude Envelope Wrapping
patterns.description: Integration tests wrap fixture JSON in a mock response envelope via make_claude_envelope(content, cost): {"is_error": false, "total_cost_usd": 5.00, "structured_output": {<fixture content>}}. This matches the real claude -p --output-format json response shape. run_agent() extracts .structured_output from this envelope. When creating new integration tests, always wrap fixtures this way — unit tests that call merge.sh functions directly do NOT need the envelope.

conventions.naming: Files named after their pipeline stage: output-docs.json (Pass 1), output-tooling.json (Pass 2), output.json (merged). Skill names are kebab-case strings in both the 'name' field and the YAML frontmatter embedded in 'content'. Hook filenames use kebab-case with .sh extension. MCP server names use lowercase (e.g. 'context7').
conventions.imports: No imports — these are static JSON data files. Consumed via jq in shell scripts: jq '.skills[]' output.json or via cat + make_claude_envelope() in bats tests.
conventions.error_handling: No error handling within fixtures themselves. The schemas (synthesis-output.json, synthesis-docs.json, synthesis-tooling.json) use additionalProperties: false to reject malformed fixtures at the schema validation layer. Missing required fields (claude_md, skills, hooks) will cause run_agent() to fail with a jq extraction error, surfaced in .ultrainit/logs/*.stderr.

internal: schemas/synthesis-output.json — JSON Schema the merged output.json must conform to
schemas/synthesis-docs.json — JSON Schema output-docs.json must conform to
schemas/synthesis-tooling.json — JSON Schema output-tooling.json must conform to
lib/synthesize.sh — reads and writes these files; merge_synthesis_passes() produces output.json
lib/merge.sh — reads output.json to write .claude/ artifacts to disk
lib/validate.sh — validates claude_md, skills, and hooks fields from output.json
tests/helpers/mock_claude.bash — wraps fixture content in Claude response envelope for integration tests
tests/helpers/test_helper.bash — provides make_claude_envelope() and tmpdir setup
tests/unit/merge.bats — primary consumer of output.json fixture
tests/unit/synthesize_helpers.bats — tests merge_synthesis_passes() using output-docs.json + output-tooling.json
tests/integration/synthesize_phase.bats — end-to-end synthesis phase test using all three fixtures

external_key.name: jq
external_key.purpose: All fixture consumption is via jq — field extraction, merging (jq -s '.[0] * .[1]'), and validation

external_key.name: bats-core
external_key.purpose: Test runner that loads these fixtures in @setup or within test cases

domain_terms.meaning: First synthesis pass that generates CLAUDE.md files only. Output shape: {claude_md, subdirectory_claude_mds}. Corresponds to output-docs.json fixture.
Second synthesis pass that generates all tooling artifacts. Output shape: {skills, hooks, subagents, mcp_servers, settings_hooks}. Corresponds to output-tooling.json fixture.
The key in the real claude -p --output-format json response envelope that contains the agent's JSON result. mock_claude.bash wraps fixture content under this key.

Entries that wire hook scripts into .claude/settings.json. Each entry has event, command (path to .sh file), and optional matcher. Distinct from the hooks[] array which contains the script content itself.
Array of {path, content} objects representing CLAUDE.md files to write into subdirectories of the target project (e.g. src/CLAUDE.md). Separate from the root claude_md string.
Test helper function that wraps fixture JSON in the {is_error, total_cost_usd, structured_output} shape matching real claude -p output.
synthesize.sh function that runs jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' on output-docs.json and output-tooling.json to produce output.json.

gotchas.issue: output.json is a derived file — it's the jq merge of output-docs.json and output-tooling.json. If you update one of the pass fixtures, you must manually re-merge to keep output.json in sync, or tests that read output.json directly will use stale data.
gotchas.solution: After editing output-docs.json or output-tooling.json, regenerate output.json with: jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' tests/fixtures/synthesis/output-docs.json tests/fixtures/synthesis/output-tooling.json > tests/fixtures/synthesis/output.json

gotchas.issue: Skill and subagent description fields in fixtures must not contain angle brackets (< >). Adding a description like 'Use for <api> endpoints' will cause validate-skill.sh to report a YAML-breaking character, and postprocess_descriptions() will silently replace them — but the replacement happens at runtime, not in the fixture. A fixture with angle brackets will still pass mock Claude dispatch but will diverge from what production would produce.
gotchas.solution: Write all description fields in plain natural language without angle brackets. Use quotes or parentheses for groupings: 'Use for "api" endpoints' or 'Use for API (REST) endpoints'.

gotchas.issue: The subagents field in output-tooling.json (and output.json) is an empty array []. Tests that assert on subagents array length will pass trivially — they don't exercise the subagent write path. If you add a test for write_artifacts() that checks subagent file creation, you need to add a subagent entry to output-tooling.json AND re-merge output.json.
gotchas.solution: Add a minimal subagent entry with name, description, and content (with YAML frontmatter) to output-tooling.json, then re-merge.

gotchas.issue: The hook in output-tooling.json uses 'Write|Edit' as the matcher, but the corresponding settings_hooks entry uses only 'Write'. This intentional mismatch is not caught by any current test — it's a documentation/fixture accuracy issue, not a schema violation.
gotchas.solution: If adding tests for hook-to-settings-wiring consistency, note that this mismatch exists in the fixture and either fix it or document the test's expectation explicitly.

gotchas.issue: Unit tests (merge.bats) copy output.json directly into $WORK_DIR/synthesis/ without wrapping in an envelope. Integration tests (synthesize_phase.bats) wrap output-docs.json and output-tooling.json in make_claude_envelope(). Using the wrong fixture + wrapping combination will cause jq to fail silently on .structured_output extraction.
gotchas.solution: Unit tests: use output.json raw. Integration tests: wrap output-docs.json / output-tooling.json with make_claude_envelope(). Never wrap output.json — it's the post-merge artifact, not a Claude response.

commands.purpose: Regenerate output.json after editing either pass fixture
Pretty-print and validate JSON syntax of the merged fixture
Run unit tests that consume these fixtures (merge.bats, synthesize_helpers.bats)
Run full test suite including integration tests that wrap these fixtures in mock Claude envelopes

skill_opportunities.name: add-synthesis-fixture
skill_opportunities.description: Guides a developer through adding a new field or entry to the synthesis fixtures while keeping all three files consistent. Useful when extending the synthesis schema with a new artifact type.
workflow_steps: Identify which schema (synthesis-docs.json or synthesis-tooling.json) needs updating
Add the new field/entry to the appropriate pass fixture (output-docs.json or output-tooling.json)
Verify no angle brackets in description fields
Update any corresponding test assertions in merge.bats or synthesize_helpers.bats
